description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, revealing a dark void or shadow between the raised stone ridges. The stone itself has a blue-gray, marbled texture, suggesting it is made from a dense, durable material such as granite or marble. The sides of the block are smooth and solid black, contrasting with the textured and patterned top. The block is thick and robust, likely intended to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. There are no visible signs of wear, markings, or engravings, and the block appears to be a single, solid piece.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its blue-gray marbled top and solid black sides, forming a striped pattern.
description_view_0: The front view shows the striped, grooved top surface with the grooves running horizontally and the solid black face below.
description_view_1: This side view displays the grooves running vertically, emphasizing the depth and spacing of the channels.
description_view_2: The rear view mirrors the front, with horizontal grooves and a solid black lower section.
description_view_3: The opposite side view again shows vertical grooves, similar to view 1, highlighting the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or decorative obstacle
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surfaces appear clean and unworn, with sharp edges and no discoloration.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
